core_mechanism: cobordism-theoretic classification (Thom) used as an algebraic-topological obstruction detecting whether a homology cycle admits a manifold representative (Steenrod realizability), yielding invariants that differ on general simplicial complexes versus manifolds
brief_reason: The abstract classifies the phases via ordinary cohomology plus beyond-cohomology contributions and recasts them through Thom's cobordism theory answering Steenrod's question of homology cycles without manifold representatives, with Dijkgraaf-Witten invariants that are nontrivial on simplicial complexes but trivial on manifolds.
